As one of 25 children, she set her sights on science is Associate Professor of Physics at the University of Yaound├⌐ 1 in Cameroon, where she is working on reducing friction on material surfaces. Her research on the mechanics of friction-and-wear processes on the molecular level has the potential to reduce the cost of energy and material losses caused by wear and can result in greater energy efficiency. Her primary goal is to make it possible to switch friction on and off in the near future, just as we can flip light switches on and off.
